channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump targets California water policy as he prepares to tour LA fire damage
item.description: As President Donald Trump prepares to tour wildfire damage in California, he's zeroing in on one of his frequent targets for criticism: State water policy. Since the fires broke out Jan.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted Arkansas OnlyFans actor arrested by US Marshals in Florida
item.description: A Van Buren porn actor who was sentenced in December for human trafficking and other charges has been arrested in Florida for violating the terms of his release, according to U.S. Marshals Service.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man arrested after allegedly climbing over wall at Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate
item.description: A man allegedly climbed over the wall that surrounds President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate in Palm Beach, Florida, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Southern cities get creative to clear leftover snow and ice after rare winter storm
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The investigation into why a Nashville high school student fatally shot a classmate before killing himself has centered on his online writings, which authorities describe as concerning warning signs.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Court upholds Amanda Knox conviction for slandering former boss in roommate's murder
item.description: Italy's Supreme Court on Thursday afternoon upheld Amanda Knox's criminal conviction for slandering her former boss, Patrick Lumumba, nearly two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Capitol visitor completes entire tour armed with gun in security failure
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police on Thursday said a man who passed a security checkpoint with a handgun and toured the chamber was arrested and charged.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Purdue Pharma and owners to pay $7.4 billion in settlement of lawsuits over the toll of OxyContin
item.description: Members of the family who own OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma, and the company itself, agreed to pay up to $7.4 billion in a new settlement to lawsuits over the toll of the powerful prescription painkiller, the attorneys general from several states announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Trump signs order to declassify files on JFK, RFK and MLK Jr. assassinations
item.description: “Give that to RFK Jr.,” Trump said about the pen he used to sign the order declassifying info about the JFK, RFK and MLK assassinations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN announced a restructuring Thursday that includes some 200 layoffs, an accelerated pivot to digital operations and new TV roles for personalities like Wolf Blitzer, Jake Tapper and Audie Cornish.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Experts worry that Trump's Jan. 6 pardons will legitimize political violence, embolden extremists
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After President Donald Trump pardoned around 1,500 Jan. 6 Capitol rioters on Monday, far-right activists cheered the move and said it strengthened their loyalty to him. Some also borrowed from the president’s own rhetoric, calling for retribution.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms John Ratcliffe as Trump's CIA director
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed John Ratcliffe to lead the Central Intelligence Agency in overwhelming bipartisan fashion, making him the second member of President Trump’s national security team to be approved by the upper chamber. Senators confirmed Ratcliffe in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: College enrollment now higher than pre-pandemic levels
item.description: Some of the biggest increases were in undergraduate certificate programs, with enrollments 28.5 percent above 2019 levels.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California will spend $2.5 billion to help the Los Angeles area recover from wildfires
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has signed a more than $2.5 billion package to help the Los Angeles area recover from its recent deadly wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What we know so far about the Antioch High School shooter
item.description: Solomon Henderson arrived at the school on a bus and is believed to have gone into the restroom and retrieved a pistol.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: Trump’s blizzard of orders gets pushback, questions from GOP lawmakers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s blizzard of executive orders during the first few days of his presidency has sent Republican lawmakers scrambling to make sense of what impact they’ll have on the country, and some GOP senators are already raising questions and concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: CNN to lay off dozens as part of major restructuring
item.description: Cable giant CNN is planning to lay off dozens of employees and undergo a major restructuring as the outlet looks to retool itself around digital during President Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's renaming plans leave mapmakers wondering what to do next
item.description: President Trump's territorial assertions sparked a round of rethinking by mapmakers and teachers, snark on social media and sarcasm by at least one other world leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump lists 'number of reasons' for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: President Donald Trump listed a “number of reasons” for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters during an interview aired Wednesday night with Fox News’s Sean Hannity.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Lawsuit claims Indiana Catholic school turned blind eye to child porn scheme; failed to protect students
item.description: And the lawsuit also claims that Bishop Luers officials not only knew all about the scheme - supposedly perpetrated by at least three boys and involving dozens of girls - but failed to report it to authorities while also trying to stonewall giving any updates of a supposed investigation to the girls
item.dc:creator: Jeff Wiehe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look for favorable jurors on charges he fired gun at former friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nearly a hundred jurors packed into a Los Angeles courtroom again on Wednesday with the possibility that they’ll serve on the trial of A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: Meta is pushing back on claims that Facebook and Instagram users are being forced into following accounts for President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Trump’s blanket Jan. 6 pardons stun Republicans on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons of more than 1,500 people charged with crimes related to the Capitol riot of Jan. 6, 2021, including individuals who assaulted police officers, stunned Republican lawmakers who witnessed firsthand the chaos on Capitol Hill four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: China and US partners are moving closer as Trump returns to the White House
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's relations are starting to improve with Japan, India and other countries that former U.S. President Joe Biden courted, just as Donald Trump brings his more unilateralist approach back to the White House.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5
